name: AWS Baseline
description: Protects critical AWS infrastructure: guards S3 bucket deletion, IAM destructive changes, KMS key scheduling, CloudTrail tampering, and compute/database termination. Requires admin approval via access request before any of these operations proceed.

rules.name: Require access request to disable or delete CloudTrail
rules.description: Stopping or deleting CloudTrail trails eliminates the audit log, which is a key indicator of compromise or insider threat. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request for S3 bucket removal and recursive delete
rules.description: aws s3 rb removes entire S3 buckets; aws s3 rm --recursive deletes all objects under a prefix. Both operations cause irreversible data loss. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request to schedule KMS key deletion
rules.description: Scheduling a KMS key for deletion permanently destroys encryption keys, making encrypted data irrecoverable. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request for IAM destructive operations
rules.description: Deleting IAM users, roles, policies, or groups can break service permissions and lock out legitimate users. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request to delete RDS instances
rules.description: Deleting an RDS instance causes data loss unless a final snapshot is taken. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request to terminate EC2 instances
rules.description: EC2 termination permanently destroys instance storage. Requires admin approval.

rules.name: Require justification when operating with a prod AWS profile
rules.description: Any AWS CLI command using a prod-named profile operates against production infrastructure. Requires a written reason.
